title: Unique Nutty Saudi Arabian Fish Pilaf
description: A kid-friendly Saudi Arabian dish featuring khus khus and a cheese-pull, with an al-dente finish, served in a ramekin. This pescatarian-friendly recipe combines the flavors of the Middle East with a nutty twist.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a medium saucepan, bring the water to a boil.
Add the basmati rice to the saucepan and reduce the heat to low.
Cover the saucepan with a tight-fitting lid and simmer for 15 minutes.
In a large skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook until softened.
Add the fish fillets to the skillet and cook until opaque and flaky.
In a small bowl, mix together the toasted khus khus, sliced almonds, salt, and black pepper.
Stir the khus khus mixture into the cooked rice.
Spoon the rice mixture into 4 ramekins.
Top each ramekin with a piece of fish, some shredded mozzarella cheese, and a sprinkle of khus khus mixture.
Bake the ramekins in the preheated oven for 10 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
